data.title: Corpay CFO Tom Panther to step down in 2025
data.description:
• Corpay (NYSE:CPAY) announced that its CFO, Tom Panther, is set to leave the company on March 15, 2025, to join the National Christian Foundation as their CFO.
  • The firm has begun the process of finding a new CFO to take his place.
  • Panther has pledged to support a smooth transition to the next CFO.
  • In addition to this executive change, it is currently expecting its fourth quarter 2024 organic revenue & earnings results to be in line with the guidance provided on November 7, 2024.

data.title: Haemonetics divests its whole blood assets to GVS for $67.1M
data.description:
• Haemonetics (NYSE:HAE) has announced the sale of its whole blood assets to GVS, S.p.A for up to $67.1M, including $44.6M upfront and potential earn-outs of $22.5M over four years.
  • The company intends to use the proceeds from this transaction for general corporate purposes and additional investments in growth initiatives.
  • The deal includes Haemonetics' whole blood collection, processing and filtration solutions, along with manufacturing facilities in Covina, California and related assets in Tijuana, Mexico.
  • "Our agreement with GVS stems from our long partnership and will enable a smooth transition for Haemonetics' whole blood customers worldwide while supporting our company's goals," said Chris Simon, Haemonetics' President and CEO.
  • The transaction is expected to close in Q1 2025.

data.title: Legrand buys Power Bus Way in Datacenters in North America
data.description:
• Legrand (OTCPK:LGRDY) announces that it has acquired Toronto-based Power Bus Way, a leading North American specialist in Cable Bus power busbars, for an undisclosed sum.
  • Power Bus Way employs over 120 people and generates approximately €70 million in annual revenue, primarily in the United States and Canada.
  • Power Bus Way is the fifth acquisition this year in the datacenter segment, following Netrack in India, Davenham in Ireland, Vass in Australia, and UPSistemas in Colombia.
  • "These acquisitions are a great illustration of our medium-term growth ambitions, both in essential building infrastructures and in offers related to the energy and digital transition," said Benoît Coquart, CEO of Legrand.

data.title: SpaceX considers tender offer that boosts valuation to $350 billion - report
data.description:
SpaceX (SPACE) is reportedly considering plans to sell existing shares at a price that could value billionaire Elon Musk's space firm at roughly $350 billion.

That would be a significant premium to a previously mulled valuation of $255 billion, and would also cement SpaceX’s status as the most valuable private startup in the world, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

SpaceX was last valued at about $210 billion in a tender offer.

The current conversations with SpaceX are ongoing and the details of a potential transaction could change depending on interest from insider sellers and buyers, the report said.

"The so-called tender or secondary offering, through which employees and some early shareholders can sell shares, provides investors in the closely held SpaceX a way to generate liquidity amid deepening ties between Musk and President-elect Donald Trump."

Tesla (TSLA) shares meanwhile are up 42% since President-elect Trump’s victory as the expectation for a relaxed regulatory environment is more favorable to the EV maker than its competitors, in both electric vehicle production and autonomous vehicles.

Elsewhere, a Delaware judge has again rejected Tesla CEO Elon Musk’s multi-billion dollar pay package for a second time, leaving the company uncertain as to how to compensate their CEO for a 1,500% share price gain over the last 5 years.

data.title: HIVE projects 15 EH/s by summer 2025
data.description:
• HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) Tuesday reported the acquisition of 8.6 EH/s of Bitmain S21+ Hydros.
  • This purchase will increase HIVE's Bitcoin mining hashrate to 15 EH/s of high-margin, self-mining capacity by summer 2025.
  • "Once fully deployed, our strategic expansion to 15 EH/s will propel HIVE to over $300 million in annualized revenue and deliver more than $200 million in mining margin, based on current mining economics," Frank Holmes, Executive Chairman of HIVE said.
  • At 2% of the Bitcoin network's total hashrate, HIVE (HIVE) expects to produce approximately 9 Bitcoin per day at the current difficulty level.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Asia markets mixed on latest U.S. trade measures blacklisting 140 more Chinese entities
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets trade mostly higher on Tuesday, led by an outperforming tech sector following record highs on Wall Street overnight. Meanwhile, investors grew cautious amid rising trade tensions between the US and China. The US announced new restrictions on China's access to critical components for chips and AI, following Trump’s renewed threat to impose 100% tariffs on BRICS nations, including China.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose +2.01% surged 1.3% to surpass 39,000, while the broader Topix Index rose 0.9% to 2,740 on Tuesday, extending gains from the previous session, with technology stocks driving the rally, the Japanese yen edged toward 150 per dollar on Tuesday, weighed down by overall dollar strength. Investors also kept a close eye on currency movements as the yen continued to strengthen.

China (SHCOMP) dipped 0.30% to fall below 3,360, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.5% to 10,700 on Tuesday, reversing some of the gains from the previous session as technology and financial stocks came under pressure. The declines followed the US's third crackdown in three years on China's semiconductor industry, restricting exports to 140 companies to limit China’s access to US technology.

On Monday, PBOC Governor Pan signaled year-end rate cuts and plans to boost countercyclical measures next year.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose +0.33% to 19,437 in the Tuesday morning session, shifting from rises in the prior two sessions after the US imposed curbs on the sale of 24 types of manufacturing equipment and three software tools, while also blacklisting 140 more Chinese entities.

India (SENSEX) rose +0.69% to 80,577, hitting their highest level in over six weeks during morning trade on Tuesday. The market extended its gains for a third consecutive session, mainly supported by gains from oil & gas, banking sectors, and financial services.

Australia (AS51) rose +0.56% rose 0.56% to close at 8,495 on Tuesday, reaching new record highs, driven by gains in mining and banking stocks.

Australia's current account deficit decreased to AUD 14.1 billion in Q3 2024 from an upwardly revised AUD 16.4 billion shortfall in Q2, indicating the sixth consecutive quarter of deficit despite missing market expectations of an AUD 10.0 billion gap.

Investors are also anticipating Australian economic growth data for the third quarter, due later this week.

The benchmark KOSPI index climbed 1.2% to 2,485 points on Tuesday, rebounding from losses in the previous session as traders assessed South Korea's latest inflation data. The country’s annual inflation rate inched higher to 1.5% in November 2024 from a 45-month low of 1.3% in the previous month, although below market expectations of 1.7%.

In the U.S., on Monday, all three major indexes ended mixed following comments from Fed Governor Christopher Waller suggesting the possibility of a 25 bps rate cut in the central bank's upcoming policy decision later this month.

U.S. stock futures remained steady on Tuesday after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite reached new record highs to kick off December: Dow +0.02%; S&P 500 +0.06%; Nasdaq +0.04%.

Job openings data is due on Tuesday, followed by initial jobless claims on Thursday and the November non-farm payrolls report on Friday. Markets are also focused on speeches from several Federal Reserve officials, with Fed Chair Jerome Powell scheduled to speak late Wednesday.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

Japan Manufacturing PMI declines to 49.0 in November, confirmed at 8-month low

Australia's retail sales rise more than expected in October by 0.6% M/M

China's manufacturing PMI expands for second consecutive month in November to 51.5

Global arms sales rise on demand linked to Ukraine and Gaza wars, Asia tensions

China factory activity grows for 2nd month; services PMI unexpectedly falls in November

data.title: Tuesday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
Auto Sales

10:00 AM Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey

A survey done by the US Bureau of Labor Statistics collects data from employers about their businesses' employment, job openings, recruitment, hires and separations. October job openings are expected to be 7.49 million from 7.443 million in September.

11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement (Preliminary)

The Treasury conducts buybacks to improve liquidity for certain maturities, limit variations in auction sizes, and reduce maturity peaks in outstanding debt.

12:35 PM Fed’s Kugler: "Labor Market and Monetary Policy"

Federal Reserve Board Governor Adriana Kugler to speak on "Labor Market and Monetary Policy" before the Detroit Economic Club.

3:45 PM Fed’s Goolsbee: "The Changing Landscape for Agricultural Inputs"

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee to give closing remarks before hybrid Midwest Agriculture Conference, "The Changing Landscape for Agricultural Inputs"
